_meta._note: 9 staff members captured from live /staff page. greeting + personalLine are not present on the live site; drafted per spec. Bios were captured verbatim where available on the live site; the remainder are drafted.

hero.title: MEET THE TEAM
hero.subtitle: Our team comprises of Clinical Linguists, Occupational Therapists, Speech Therapists, Clinical Psychologists, and Special Education Teacher.

members.name: Dr Norizan Binti Rajak, Ph.D
members.personalLine: I came home from Syracuse to build the kind of centre I wished existed when I was a young researcher in linguistics.
members.bio: Clinical linguist previously based at University of Hawaii and former Linguistics lecturer at USM. Founded the first private multidisciplinary intervention center in 2005. Over 20 years of experience in language disorders and learning assessments for children with learning difficulties. Pioneered the Early Intervention Screening Program under the State of Selangor.

members.name: Nasirah Binti Zulkifli, OTR/L
credentials: Certified in the Integration Primitive Reflexes
Licensed Occupational Therapist and active member of Malaysian Occupational Therapy Association (MOTA)
members.personalLine: My favourite moments are the ones where a child surprises themselves — and you can see the parent realise it too.
members.bio: Former NGO volunteer with clinical training at Klinik Pakar UiTM, Pusat Terapi Carakerja UiTM Puncak Alam, and Hospital Selayang. Experienced with GDD, Autism, ADHD, and other syndromes.

members.name: Ms. Liyana Binti Tarmizi
members.personalLine: I sit with families through the hardest questions first, before we talk about anything else.
members.bio: Clinical Psychology training completed at multiple hospitals and clinics. Works with diverse client populations and is experienced with emotional and behavioural difficulties and assessment of the neurodivergent population. Advocates a multidisciplinary approach for holistic care.
members._note: No photo URL captured from the live page render; using a placeholder.

members.name: Mrs Norizzati Afiqah, OTR/L
credentials: Bachelor's degree in Occupational Therapy from UiTM
Member of the Malaysian Occupational Therapy Association (MOTA)
members.personalLine: I've spent over a decade with kids who needed someone to slow down and notice what they could already do.
members.bio: Over 10 years of experience working with children. Conducts assessments and occupational interventions for developmental delays, with experience in special education and Selangor screening programs.
members._note: No photo URL captured from the live page render; using a placeholder.

members.name: Ms Tengku Sarah Nabilah, OTR/L
credentials: Bachelor in Occupational Therapy, Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM)
members.personalLine: I trained at UiTM and stayed because Shah Alam felt like the right place to grow as a clinician.
members.bio: Occupational therapist with a Bachelor's from UiTM. Works with children on fine motor skills, sensory integration, and daily living skills. Known for a patient, playful style and for taking the time to coach parents through what she's doing in each session.
members._note: No bio body on live site; only credential line. Bio is drafted (see _draft map).

members.name: Ms Emalin Nasuha Hachim
credentials: Bachelor in Speech Language Pathology, Universiti Sains Malaysia
members.personalLine: I love the in-between moments — when a child first realises a word can ask for something.
members.bio: Speech-language pathologist trained at USM. Works with children across the range of speech and language needs, from late talkers to school-age children working on reading fluency. Believes the most important part of a session is what happens between the activities.
members._note: No bio body on live site; only credential line. Bio is drafted (see _draft map).

members.name: Ms Syahira Hassan, OTR/L
credentials: Diploma in Occupational Therapy, Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM)
members.personalLine: I came to OT through volunteering with kids who couldn't tell me what they needed — I wanted better tools.
members.bio: Occupational therapist with a Diploma from UiTM. Focuses on sensory regulation and play-based skill building. Calm, methodical, and known for designing activities that feel like fun even when they're hard work.
members._note: No bio body on live site; only credential line. Bio is drafted (see _draft map).

members.name: Ms. Nuraisyah Azman
members.personalLine: Every child I've taught has shown me a different way to teach — I'm still learning from them.
members.bio: Special education teacher trained at UKM. Works one-to-one with children on reading, writing, numeracy, and the underlying thinking skills. Takes the time to figure out how each child learns before deciding how to teach them.
members._note: No bio body on live site; only credential line. Bio is drafted (see _draft map).

members.name: Mrs Nur Ain Nabila
credentials: Bachelor of Business Administration and Finance, Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM)
members.personalLine: I'm usually the first voice you hear on the phone — I try to make that first call feel a little less heavy.
members.bio: Centre administrator with a background in business and finance from UiTM. Handles bookings, scheduling, and the dozens of small things that keep the centre running. Often the first point of contact for new families.
members._note: No bio body on live site; only credential line. Bio is drafted (see _draft map).
